rationale: The citing paper uses Lewis (2004) to support a core framing claim that threshold-based loyalty rewards create forward-looking purchase considerations, not just immediate price responses. It also places Lewis (2004) in its literature map of dynamic, single-program, rational-choice loyalty research and positions the current study as moving beyond that stream to competing programs and bounded rationality. Although cited alongside other papers, the citation helps define the theoretical baseline and contribution space rather than serving as a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Lewis (2004) as prior dynamic loyalty-program evidence and a key reference point for the single-program structural tradition. It builds from that baseline by studying consumer decisions under competing loyalty programs and bounded rationality in an experimental setting.
